parsed_review.summary: CP00-920 is a descriptor-only RP30 People-HR HRX bridge pack over RP30.P03.M02.S02-RP30.P03.M04.S01 (10 implementation units: 4 LeaveApi tail rows in M02.S02-S05, 5 CandidateApi foundation rows in M03.S01-S05, 1 EvidenceApi foundation row in M04.S01). Read-only inspection confirms the production_ready boundary is supported: registry.js builds all 10 rows with descriptor_only:true and every runtime/payload/record-write/real-data/secret/separate-product/enterprise-trust field hard-coded false (a grep for *_opened/_included/_written/_executed:true returned no matches); validators.js (coverage + descriptor + contract) enforce every one of those boundaries plus capability/safety-gate/no-leak-guard coverage and reject Claude-as-final-approver and Hermes runtime-receipt emission; model.test.js asserts the CP00-920 coverage distribution and closed boundaries with validation.errors deep-equal []; the generated contracts/hrx-people-contract.json carries latest_pack/projection=CP00-920, validation.valid=true, and a holding no-write attestation with no drift versus the registry; and scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs chains the full binding/coverage/descriptor/contract ladder. Authority boundary is correct: Claude is a read-only independent reviewer (not final approver), the Hermes packet is deterministic review-packet/evidence metadata only with emits_runtime_receipt:false and is not embedded in product runtime, and the readiness auth preflight passed (1233-byte valid JSON stdout, is_error:false) — no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced/tool-shaped evidence, or missing fields. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Two non-blocking P3s: (1) the pack-level review artifacts (adjudication.md verdict PENDING, construction-inspection.json production_ready:false with runtime_ready/status/boundary null, claude-review-result.json pending_review, manifest pack_level_claude_review pending with empty valid_review_receipts and no receipt/raw-output yet) are in the sanctioned pre-promotion state and must be normalized from this single review before production_ready flips; (2) command-evidence.json exit codes are agent-reported and were verified by structural inspection (scripts exist in package.json, validation script and tests are sound) but not re-executed under the read-only constraint. Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block pack closeout or this CP closeout goal. The descriptor-only pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim, so final human approval for enterprise trust correctly remains outside this receipt.

findings.title: Post-review adjudication, construction inspection, and review receipt normalization still pending
findings.details: At review time the pack-level review artifacts are in their expected pre-promotion state, not a final state: adjudication.md reads 'Overall verdict: PENDING' / 'Production ready after adjudication: no'; construction-inspection.json has production_ready:false with runtime_ready/status/boundary all null; claude-review-result.json status is 'pending_review' with null review/findings/dispositions; and manifest.json pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending_review' with overall_verdict 'PENDING', empty valid_review_receipts, and null blocks_* flags. No review-receipt.json or raw-output.json exists for cp00-920 yet. This is the sanctioned pre-review state (mirrors CP00-919 finding CP919-P3-01) and not a code or boundary defect, but the pack cannot be promoted to descriptor-only production_ready until closeout automation records exactly one valid hardened read-only review receipt from this review and normalizes the adjudication, inspection, claude-review-result, and manifest review block accordingly.
findings.recommendation: Have closeout automation consume this review receipt, then normalize adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, claude-review-result.json, and manifest.pack_level_claude_review from this single review (exactly one valid receipt, no blocking findings) and re-run the full deterministic validation ladder before flipping production_ready to true.

findings.title: Closeout command exit codes are agent-reported, not re-executed under read-only review
findings.details: command-evidence.json records six commands (node --check on registry/validators/validate script; node --test packages/hrx/test/*.test.js; npm run rp30:hrx:validate, rp30:validate, hrx:requirements:validate, hrx:weighted:validate) all at exit_code 0. Under the read-only review constraint (Read/Grep/Glob only, no Bash) these exit codes were not independently re-executed. Provenance distinction: the implementation and structure were verified directly — the referenced npm scripts exist in package.json:128-133, scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs soundly chains binding/coverage/descriptor/contract checks, and packages/hrx/test/model.test.js asserts the CP00-920 boundaries with validation.errors deep-equal [] — but the command pass/fail itself is reported, not re-run in this session. The prompt's full closeout ladder (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) likewise cannot be executed here.
findings.recommendation: Closeout automation with execution rights must run the full validation ladder and capture exit codes before promotion; treat the recorded exit codes as evidence to be re-confirmed by the deterministic harness rather than as reviewer-verified results.
